title: eupatorium altissimum
intro: Eupatorium altissimum, also known as the tall boneset or great joe-pye weed, is a flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Eupatorium altissimum are believed to provide various health benefits, including relief from fever, pain, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Eupatorium altissimum are typically made from its leaves and flowers, which can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
